info.brief: Dicebag is a Defold extension containing probability functions designed specifically for games.
info.description: Dicebag is a Defold extension containing probability functions designed specifically for games.

elements.name: set_up_rng
elements.brief: Sets up the randomseed and clears the first number of random rolls.
elements.description: Sets up the randomseed and clears the first number of random rolls.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.doc: optional seed, if not specified a seed will be generated using socket.gettime().

returnvalues.doc: the number used to seed the random function.

elements.name: roll_dice
elements.brief: Roll a number of dice, D&D-style. An example would be rolling 3d6+2. Returns the sum of the resulting roll.
elements.description: Roll a number of dice, D&D-style. An example would be rolling 3d6+2. Returns the sum of the resulting roll.

parameters.name: num_dice
parameters.doc: Number of dice to roll.

parameters.name: num_sides
parameters.doc: Number of sides on the dice.

parameters.name: modifier
parameters.doc: Number to add to the result.

returnvalues.doc: Sum of rolled dice plus modifier.

elements.name: roll_special_dice
elements.brief: Roll a number of dice and choose one (or more) of the highest (advantage) or lowest (disadvantage) results. Returns the sum of the relevant dice rolls.
elements.description: Roll a number of dice and choose one (or more) of the highest (advantage) or lowest (disadvantage) results. Returns the sum of the relevant dice rolls.

parameters.name: num_sides
parameters.doc: Number of sides on the dice.

parameters.name: advantage
parameters.doc: If true, the highest rolls will be selected, otherwise the lowest values will be selected.

parameters.name: num_dice
parameters.doc: Number of dice to roll.

parameters.name: num_results
parameters.doc: How many of the highest (advantage) or lowest (disadvantage) dice to sum up.

returnvalues.doc: Sum of the highest (advantage) or lowest (disadvantage) dice rolls

elements.name: roll_custom_dice
elements.brief: Roll custom dice. The dice can have sides with different weights and different values.
elements.description: Roll custom dice. The dice can have sides with different weights and different values.

parameters.name: num_dice
parameters.doc: How many dice to roll.

parameters.name: sides
parameters.doc: A table describing the sides of the die in the format `[[weight1, value1], [weight2, value2], ...]`.

returnvalues.doc: The sum of the values as specified in the table `sides`.

elements.name: bag_create
elements.brief: Create a marble bag of green (success) and red (fails) 'marbles'. This allows you to, for example, make an unlikely event more and more likely the more fails are accumulated.
elements.description: Create a marble bag of green (success) and red (fails) 'marbles'. This allows you to, for example, make an unlikely event more and more likely the more fails are accumulated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.doc: A unique identifier for the marble bag.

parameters.name: num_success
parameters.doc: The number of success marbles in the bag.

parameters.name: num_fails
parameters.doc: The number of fails marbles in the bag.

parameters.name: reset_on_success
parameters.doc: Whether or not the bag should reset when a successful result is drawn. If false or nil the bag will reset when all marbles have been drawn.

elements.name: bag_draw
elements.brief: Draw a marble from a previously created bag.
elements.description: Draw a marble from a previously created bag.

parameters.name: id
parameters.doc: A unique identifier for the marble bag.

elements.name: bag_reset
elements.brief: Manually reset a marble bag. Will also be called when a marble bag is empty, or a success is drawn in a bag where `reset_on_success` is true.
elements.description: Manually reset a marble bag. Will also be called when a marble bag is empty, or a success is drawn in a bag where `reset_on_success` is true.

parameters.name: id
parameters.doc: A unique identifier for the marble bag.

elements.name: table_create
elements.brief: Create a rollable table. This is similar to a marble bag, except each entry can have a different weight, and can return any value (not just a boolean).
elements.description: Create a rollable table. This is similar to a marble bag, except each entry can have a different weight, and can return any value (not just a boolean).

parameters.name: id
parameters.doc: A unique identifier for the rollable table.

parameters.name: rollable_table
parameters.doc: A table of weights, values and reset flags.

elements.name: table_roll
elements.brief: Draw a random value from the rollable table created in `table_create`. The value will be removed from the table. If `reset_on_roll` is true, the table will reset. Otherwise, the table will reset when all values are drawn.
elements.description: Draw a random value from the rollable table created in `table_create`. The value will be removed from the table. If `reset_on_roll` is true, the table will reset. Otherwise, the table will reset when all values are drawn.

parameters.name: id
parameters.doc: A unique identifier for the rollable table.

elements.name: table_reset
elements.brief: Manually reset a rollable table. Will also be called when the rollable table is empty, or a drawn value where `reset_on_roll` is true.
elements.description: Manually reset a rollable table. Will also be called when the rollable table is empty, or a drawn value where `reset_on_roll` is true.

parameters.name: id
parameters.doc: A unique identifier for the rollable table.
